commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a public plugin-sdk outbound delivery-report subpath, routes canonical message_sent/message:sent emission through a shared core helper, and wires WhatsApp native auto-replies to report provider-accepted sends.

PR surface: Source +272, Tests +142, Docs +1, Config +4, Other +1. Total +420 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main emits sent hooks from the shared outbound path while WhatsApp native auto-reply sends through provider-local methods; no live WhatsApp account was run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed for contributor real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the new public SDK contract; no narrow automated repair remains in the current head.

target.title: feat(plugin-sdk): generic outbound delivery-report seam; WhatsApp native auto-reply reports provider-accepted sends
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Channel-self-delivered sends (native auto-replies) bypass core `deliverOutboundPayloads`, so the canonical `message:sent` event never fires and `message:sent` consumers (the `message_sent` plugin hook + the internal `message:sent` hook) are blind to them despite successful delivery. Today there is no extension-facing way to report such a delivery (`emitMessageSent`/`createInternalHookEvent`/`getGlobalHookRunner` are core-internal).

## Why This Change Was Made
Core owns the canonical `message.sent` emission; extensions need a narrow, generic, supported way to report a delivery they performed without reaching into core hook internals. This adds one shared core emitter (`emitCanonicalMessageSent`) that both the explicit-send path and the new seam route through, so the two emission sites cannot drift in hook order or gating.

## User Impact
Plugins/consumers observing `message_sent` / internal `message:sent` now also observe channel-self-delivered messages (e.g. delivery logs, responded-accounting, watchdogs). The explicit-send path is unchanged. Additive and fail-open — no change to delivery behavior or config.

## Evidence
New `src/infra/outbound/report-outbound-delivered.test.ts` (Vitest): emits exactly one canonical `message:sent`; idempotent on `messageId` / `channel`+`to`+`correlationId`; fails open (no throw, internal hook skipped) when `sessionKey` is absent; never throws when a downstream hook handler throws. The `deliver.ts` refactor is behavior-preserving (delegates to the shared emitter; removes the now-duplicated emission). Touched files typecheck clean locally (scoped); deferring to CI/tsgo for the authoritative run.

Closes #97726
target.author: mids-neo
